expectations.text: Recommends <caption> element for the table description
expectations.evidence: '<caption> over aria-label — the caption is visible, native, and universally supported by screen readers'

expectations.text: Uses <thead> with <th scope='col'> for week column headers
expectations.evidence: scope='col' on week headers in <thead> explicitly mentioned

expectations.text: Uses <th scope='row'> for the player name column
expectations.evidence: scope='row' on player name cells in <tbody> explicitly mentioned with note about VoiceOver on iOS

expectations.text: Addresses the '—' bye week cell — needs accessible text (aria-label or visually hidden text), not just a dash character
expectations.evidence: aria-hidden='true' on dash + visually-hidden bye — correct pattern

expectations.text: Advises against role=grid since the table is read-only data (not interactive)
expectations.evidence: 'Do NOT use role=grid — that pattern is for interactive, editable grids and requires full arrow-key keyboard management'

expectations.text: Addresses abbreviated column headers (e.g. 'Wk 1') — recommends <abbr> or aria-label for full text
expectations.evidence: Not explicitly addressed in the summary; scope and bye week covered but abbreviated headers not mentioned

expectations.text: Covers a keyboard alternative interaction (pick up / move / drop / cancel)
expectations.evidence: Parallel keyboard interaction built separately from mouse drag events — Space/Enter to pick up, Tab/arrows to navigate, Enter to drop, Escape to cancel

expectations.text: Mentions Space or Enter to pick up and drop, arrow keys to move
expectations.evidence: Enter/Space to pick up, arrow keys for roving tabindex within available players, Enter to drop

expectations.text: Mentions Escape to cancel a drag operation
expectations.evidence: Escape cancels the operation, returns focus to player list

expectations.text: Flags that aria-grabbed is deprecated and should not be used
expectations.evidence: 'Do not use aria-grabbed alone... It was deprecated in ARIA 1.1 because it was inconsistently supported. Use it as a supplement, but rely on aria-selected and live region announcements as the primary mechanism' — correctly demotes it

expectations.text: Covers live region announcements for drag state (picked up, moved to, dropped)
expectations.evidence: announce() helper with textContent='' + requestAnimationFrame trick; scripts what to say at each stage

expectations.text: Mentions visible indication of the 'grabbed' / active drag state
expectations.evidence: CSS for drag mode and valid drop targets mentioned in testing checklist

expectations.text: Mentions providing instructions to users on how to use the keyboard interface
expectations.evidence: Keyboard instructions are part of the recommended UX; flagged in WCAG anti-patterns section

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend role=menu for this use case
expectations.evidence: Explicitly rejects role=menu: 'Semantically incorrect. menu/menuitem is for application-style action menus, not navigation'

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend dialog for this use case
expectations.evidence: Explicitly rejects dialog: 'Too heavy. Dialogs imply a task requiring a decision and trap focus. Switching teams is a single-step navigation action'

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend combobox for this use case
expectations.evidence: Explicitly rejects combobox: 'Wrong affordance. Combobox signals type to filter and is announced as a form input'

expectations.text: Recommends a disclosure button (<button> with aria-expanded) + list of links
expectations.evidence: Recommends button with aria-expanded paired with <ul hidden> of <a> links — the APG Disclosure pattern

expectations.text: Explains WHY role=menu is wrong (it's for app actions, not navigation links)
expectations.evidence: 'menu/menuitem is for application-style action menus (think right-click context menus), not navigation. Screen readers announce menu widgets in application mode, suppressing normal reading shortcuts'

expectations.text: Recommends using <a> elements (links) rather than <button> elements for the team options
expectations.evidence: 'Native <a> link behavior (Tab through, Enter to navigate)' — links recommended for the team options

expectations.text: Recommends <caption> element for the table description
expectations.evidence: 'Place a <caption> as the first child of <table>. It is the programmatic label for the table'

expectations.text: Uses <thead> with <th scope='col'> for week column headers
expectations.evidence: 'All column headers use <th scope="col">'

expectations.text: Uses <th scope='row'> for the player name column
expectations.evidence: 'Player name cells use <th scope="row">, not <td>. This is the critical distinction between a layout table and a data table'

expectations.text: Addresses the '—' bye week cell — needs accessible text (aria-label or visually hidden text), not just a dash character
expectations.evidence: aria-hidden on dash + sr-only 'bye' span — same correct pattern

expectations.text: Advises against role=grid since the table is read-only data (not interactive)
expectations.evidence: role=grid not addressed; no guidance on when to use or avoid it

expectations.text: Addresses abbreviated column headers (e.g. 'Wk 1') — recommends <abbr> or aria-label for full text
expectations.evidence: Abbreviated headers not mentioned

expectations.text: Covers a keyboard alternative interaction (pick up / move / drop / cancel)
expectations.evidence: Space/Enter pick up, Tab/Arrow navigate, Space/Enter drop, Escape cancel

expectations.text: Mentions Space or Enter to pick up and drop, arrow keys to move
expectations.evidence: Documented in keyboard interaction section

expectations.text: Mentions Escape to cancel a drag operation
expectations.evidence: Escape cancels and returns focus

expectations.text: Flags that aria-grabbed is deprecated and should not be used
expectations.evidence: Says 'aria-grabbed and aria-dropeffect are deprecated in ARIA 1.1 but still have good screen reader support. They are safe to use' — does not flag as avoid; actively recommends continuing use

expectations.text: Covers live region announcements for drag state (picked up, moved to, dropped)
expectations.evidence: Hidden aria-live region announces each drag state change

expectations.text: Mentions visible indication of the 'grabbed' / active drag state
expectations.evidence: CSS for drag mode and valid drop targets included

expectations.text: Mentions providing instructions to users on how to use the keyboard interface
expectations.evidence: aria-describedby linking to static keyboard instructions

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend role=menu for this use case
expectations.evidence: Incorrectly recommends role=menu: 'A role=menu triggered by a button represents a list of actions or choices — which is exactly what this control is'

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend dialog for this use case
expectations.evidence: Correctly rejects dialog as too heavy

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend combobox for this use case
expectations.evidence: Correctly rejects combobox as wrong affordance

expectations.text: Recommends a disclosure button (<button> with aria-expanded) + list of links
expectations.evidence: Recommends role=menu with menuitem children instead of disclosure + links

expectations.text: Explains WHY role=menu is wrong (it's for app actions, not navigation links)
expectations.evidence: Does the opposite — argues role=menu IS correct; does not distinguish between app menus and navigation

expectations.text: Recommends using <a> elements (links) rather than <button> elements for the team options
expectations.evidence: Recommends menuitem role (buttons) rather than <a> links for navigation items
